issues.title: Internationalization and Localization of resources
issues.body:
Currently e.g. `QuestionAnswerAdvisor` has a default English user text advise, one can override it via the constructor as followss:

```java
.defaultAdvisors(new QuestionAnswerAdvisor(vectorStore, SearchRequest.defaults(), DEFAULT_USER_TEXT_ADVISE)) // Enable RAG
```

It would be great to [internationalize](https://docs.spring.io/spring-boot/reference/features/internationalization.html) it and maybe provide the values via properties?

Another use case would be to treat different models as different "languages" which means that those could also be parameterezied.

comments.body: All advisors that deal with prompt augmentation now support passing a custom `PromptTemplate` object instead of a plain string, which makes it possible to customise the text (including the language) and the rendering logic (choosing a different `TemplateRenderer`).
Since the customisation is available from `PromptTemplate` and `TemplateRenderer`, closing this for now.

labels.name: RAG
labels.description: Issues related to Retrieval Augmented Generation

issues.title: BiFunction with ToolContext Fails Due to Incorrect Type Resolution in Spring Cloud Function

comments.body:
Hey @sfcodes, thank you for catching this and for the thoughtful analysis of the issue.   
I think if seen similar issues in the past but related to the Function interface: #726 , resolved like [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/c979238993e7d7625d4ef3f9d79d1b2012d84f1d/spring-ai-core/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/model/function/TypeResolverHelper.java#L131).

I will try to reproduce the issue and see if i can address it on spring-ai side or contact the spring cloud function team. 

Also I think we have to throw an exception if the inferred type is java.lang.Object.
